topic: Data Preprocessing: Handling Outliers, Missing Values, and Skewed Data

questions.scenario: A real-world industrial problem statement: An e-commerce company is trying to build a recommendation system using their sales data, but the dataset contains a significant number of missing values and outliers, causing the model to perform poorly.
questions.question: What is the most suitable technique for handling missing values in this dataset?
questions.explanation: Imputation using regression is a suitable technique for handling missing values in this dataset because it uses the relationship between variables to estimate the missing values, which can help to reduce bias and improve the model's performance.

questions.scenario: A real-world industrial problem statement: A company is building a predictive model to forecast energy consumption, but the dataset is highly skewed, with a few extreme values affecting the model's performance.
questions.question: What is the best approach to handle skewed data in this scenario?
questions.explanation: Log transformation is a suitable technique for handling skewed data because it can reduce the effect of extreme values and make the data more normally distributed, which can improve the model's performance.

questions.scenario: A real-world industrial problem statement: A financial institution is building a real-time credit scoring system that needs to handle high-dimensional data and make predictions within a short time frame.
questions.question: What is the most suitable algorithm for handling high-dimensional data in this real-time scenario?
questions.explanation: Principal Component Analysis (PCA) is a suitable algorithm for handling high-dimensional data in this real-time scenario because it can reduce the dimensionality of the data while retaining most of the information, which can improve the model's performance and reduce the computational time.
